key_insight: MXtalGFlow reframes molecular crystal prediction as learning a thermodynamic distribution over structures, rather than generating isolated candidate crystals.
engineering_relevance: This enables more physically meaningful crystal design workflows by sampling polymorph probabilities and revealing competitive packing modes under chosen energy models and space groups.
method: Define a canonical parameterization of molecular crystal structures.
Train energy-based Generative Flow Networks (GFlowNets) to match the Boltzmann distribution over crystals.
Analyze sampled distributions to identify basins around known polymorphs and new plausible packings.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports tests on two molecules under two energy functions (Lennard-Jones and Universal Model for Atoms), with comparisons to known experimental polymorphs and additional predicted packing modes.
watch_item: Results depend on the chosen energy model and space-group/parameterization assumptions, so distribution quality may shift if the physics model or structural constraints are imperfect.
paper_title: A Distributional Framework for Generative Modeling of Molecular Crystals
